findings.title: S3 Bucket Public Access Vulnerability
findings.description: An S3 bucket in `/infrastructure/main.tf` has configurations (e.g., CKV_AWS_24, 260, CKV2_AWS_5, 6, 62, CKV_AWS_18) that indicate it is, or could become, publicly accessible. This misconfiguration leads to unauthorized data exposure and potential data breaches, which is a severe security risk.
findings.remediation: Ensure all S3 buckets have Public Access Block (PAB) enabled for all four settings (BlockPublicAcls, IgnorePublicAcls, BlockPublicPolicy, RestrictPublicBuckets). Review bucket policies and ACLs to ensure no public access is granted. For example, in Terraform: `block_public_acls = true`, `block_public_policy = true`, `ignore_public_acls = true`, `restrict_public_buckets = true`.

findings.title: S3 Bucket Cross-Account Access
findings.description: An S3 bucket in `/infrastructure/main.tf` permits cross-account access (CKV_AWS_21). While potentially legitimate for specific integrations, this configuration must be meticulously reviewed to align with least privilege principles and prevent unintended data exposure or unauthorized operations across AWS accounts.
findings.remediation: Review the S3 bucket policy and ACLs for the resource in `/infrastructure/main.tf` to identify and validate cross-account access grants. Ensure only necessary accounts have access and permissions are limited to the principle of least privilege. Remove any unauthorized or overly permissive access.

findings.title: EBS Volume Encryption Disabled
findings.description: An EBS volume (CKV_AWS_145) in `/infrastructure/main.tf` is not configured for encryption. This leaves data at rest unencrypted, increasing the risk of data compromise if the underlying EC2 instance is breached or snapshots are accessed without authorization.
findings.remediation: Ensure all EBS volumes are encrypted. This can be enforced by setting default encryption for the AWS account or by explicitly setting `encrypted = true` for `aws_ebs_volume` or within `ebs_block_device` blocks for `aws_instance` resources in Terraform.

findings.title: S3 Bucket Server-Side Encryption Disabled
findings.description: An S3 bucket in `/infrastructure/main.tf` (CKV_AWS_23) does not have explicit server-side encryption configured. While S3 encrypts data at rest by default, explicit SSE-S3 or KMS encryption provides better control, auditability, and compliance adherence for sensitive data.
findings.remediation: Configure default server-side encryption for the S3 bucket. In Terraform, add a `server_side_encryption_configuration` block to the `aws_s3_bucket` resource, specifying `SSE-S3` or `aws:kms` as the `algorithm`.

findings.title: S3 Bucket Access Logging Disabled
findings.description: An S3 bucket in `/infrastructure/main.tf` (CKV_AWS_25) lacks access logging. This prevents effective auditing and incident response, making it difficult to detect, investigate, or prove unauthorized access or data exfiltration attempts against the bucket.
findings.remediation: Enable S3 access logging for the bucket, configuring it to deliver logs to a separate, secured S3 bucket in a different account or region if possible. In Terraform, add a `logging` block to the `aws_s3_bucket` resource, specifying the `target_bucket` and `target_prefix`.

findings.title: S3 Bucket Versioning Disabled
findings.description: S3 bucket versioning (CKV2_AWS_61) is not enabled in `/infrastructure/main.tf`. While not a direct security vulnerability, versioning is crucial for data durability and recovery, protecting against accidental deletions or malicious overwrites which can lead to data integrity loss.
findings.remediation: Enable versioning for the S3 bucket. In Terraform, within the `aws_s3_bucket` resource, add or update the `versioning` block to set `enabled = true`.

findings.title: Docker Pip Cache Usage Warning
findings.description: The Dockerfile (DL3042) uses `pip install` without `--no-cache-dir`. This can lead to larger image sizes and potentially cache vulnerable packages within the image. It's a best practice to avoid unnecessary caching during production image builds for security and efficiency.
findings.remediation: Modify `pip install` commands in the Dockerfile to include the `--no-cache-dir` flag. For example: `RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt`. Also, consider using multi-stage builds to further minimize final image size.

findings.title: EC2 Instance Basic Monitoring
findings.description: EC2 instances in `/infrastructure/main.tf` (CKV_AWS_144) are configured with basic CloudWatch monitoring (5-minute data points) instead of detailed monitoring (1-minute data points). This delays the detection of critical performance issues or unusual activity that could indicate a security incident.
findings.remediation: Enable detailed monitoring for EC2 instances. In Terraform, set `monitoring = true` within the `aws_instance` resource definition or relevant launch configuration/template.

gate_reason: A critical S3 bucket public access vulnerability has been identified that requires immediate remediation.
